1: Your Wisdom stat gives you more time to answer quiz questions. Every point above 10 adds a second.
Stamina (SP) drains as you move. When it runs low, you'll grow Hungry — and eventually start losing HP.
Scrolls are consumed when read, but spellbooks can teach you a spell to cast forever.
Pressing Z opens the eat menu. Cooked food restores far more than eating things raw.
Pressing ? opens the help screen, which lists every key command in the game.
Lockpicks wear out with use. A fragile pick breaks faster when you fail to open a lock.
Gold and items are sometimes hidden inside locked chests — watch for them in dungeon rooms.
You can pick up items by standing on them and pressing G or comma.
The dungeon gets harder the deeper you go, but better equipment and ingredients appear too.
Different weapons deal different damage types — slash, pierce, or blunt. Experiment to find what works.
2: Fire creatures resist fire damage and are weak to cold. The reverse is also true for ice creatures.
Your Perception (PER) stat increases both your sight radius and the range of ranged weapons.
When confused, your quiz timer is shortened — cure confusion quickly before a fight.
Intelligence (INT) raises your MP cap and gives bonus seconds on magic-subject quizzes.
Cooking monster ingredients gives SP and HP. The deeper the dungeon the monster came from, the better the meal.
Staircase rest heals you each floor — save potions and healing herbs for when stairs are far away.
Undead creatures often resist physical damage but are weak to fire or holy effects.
Weapons with reach can attack monsters one or two tiles away — useful against dangerous melee fighters.
Wands have limited charges — use them wisely against powerful monsters or in desperate situations.
Accessories (R key) provide passive effects when equipped. Some grant elemental resistances.
Strength (STR) improves both your carrying capacity and your maximum stamina pool.
Constitution (CON) directly raises your maximum HP. Investing in CON makes you much harder to kill.
3: Wolf-type ingredients combined with deep cave fungi are said to produce meals of considerable strength.
Two healing ingredients combined in a recipe create a restorative meal more powerful than either alone.
Dragon-type ingredients, when carefully cooked, produce meals that greatly increase physical resilience.
Ingredients from flying creatures combined with herbs often create meals that sharpen the mind.
Bone-based ingredients combined with cave mushrooms are said to fortify the body's endurance.
Undead-derived ingredients, when properly prepared, can grant temporary supernatural resistances.
The deeper the dungeon, the stronger the recipe ingredients. A tier-3 recipe with tier-5 parts is exceptionally powerful.
Some compound recipes require three or more ingredients — experiment to discover the most powerful combinations.
Aquatic creature ingredients combined with underground plants can create meals that clear the mind.
Fire-type and cold-type ingredients balanced together create a meal said to improve all statistics equally.
A fire-bringer in a starlit robe, sashed in gold, is said to walk as a hidden character — his strength is titanic and his legacy burns eternal.
One hidden character owns almost nothing: bald, simply robed, and unfazed by hardship. His constitution is legendary because he demands so little of the world.
A cunning wanderer in a hood, said to have survived ten years of misadventure at sea, begins the dungeon with exceptional dexterity and adaptability.
Two female spellcasters share the same face but differ in arcane mastery. Both begin with a wand and a spellbook — one learned magic, the other mastered it.
A philosopher-mathematician in forest green, with a love of numbers and harmonics, is said to be a hidden character with a uniquely balanced mind.
There are rare, powerful creatures that don't follow the dungeon's normal rules — they appear only once per descent, if at all. Treat their appearance as both a warning and an opportunity.
A spider of unusual size and cunning lurks in the early depths. Her web immobilizes even armored warriors. Fire clears it faster than any blade.
Something half-woman and half-serpent has been reported in the depths — she drains the warmth from those she embraces. She cannot abide fire or holy energy.
A bronze giant walks slowly through certain levels, shrugging off every arrow and blade. Travelers who survived say only blunt weapons left any mark, and a strange ankle wound finally felled it.
A serpent-woman who mothers all monsters poisons with every bite. Those who have faced her and lived wear protection against venom without exception.
Reports of a forest spirit that lures wanderers to their doom — iron weapons and torches seem to disturb it. Do not follow the music.
A bat-god from the underworld has been seen blinding adventurers with its wings. Holy energy or lightning seems to hurt it where conventional arms do not.
A lone sphinx in the depths poses riddles to those bold enough to listen. Philosophy is the key to her ancient wisdom.
Ancient Greek smiths spoke of an unfinished hammer buried deep. Where thunder echoes, dwarven work awaits.
Alchemists sought the power to turn lead to gold. Their crucibles still burn in the shallower depths.
A Celtic cauldron of transformation is said to demand meals as tribute. Cook well before you seek it.
Pandora's box is said to lie somewhere in the depths. But was opening it truly a mistake?
The Wandering Jew walked forever. Fifteen thousand steps in the dungeon are said to reveal all hidden things.
Cerberus guarded the underworld's gate. Three hundred passages through stairs honors his vigilance.
The Sphinx was defeated not by strength but by answering while blind to all else.
Musashi's greatest duels were won with restraint. One strike, not many, showed his mastery.
Rasputin survived poison, bullet, and blade before finally falling. Survive on the very edge of death five times, and constitution becomes legend.
Cassandra's prophecies were true even when her methods seemed flawed. Pass a test despite making mistakes — do it ten times.
Tantalus was punished by eternal hunger. Those who persist through fifteen ruined meals find iron resolve in deprivation.
Orpheus walked among the dead with only his music. Stand beside monsters without striking them — do it long enough, five separate times.
The Buddha sat in perfect stillness beneath the bodhi tree, unmoved by every threat. Wait five hundred times in the presence of enemies.
Hephaestus returned to his forge obsessively, perfecting the same piece again and again. Equip and unequip one armor piece fifteen times.
4: Ancient texts speak of a great horned beast around the 20th depth — swift, brutal, and cunning in enclosed spaces.
The Gorgon is said to haunt passages around the 40th level. Travelers found turned to stone vanished after venturing there.
A legendary dragon sleeps near the 60th depth. Those who faced it note that slashing weapons found little purchase on its hide.
The great wolf Fenrir prowls near the 80th depth. Survivors describe a creature that can absorb tremendous punishment.
The final guardian waits at the deepest levels. Scouts report that slashing weapons are ineffective against its form — pierce or magic may be necessary.
Bosses tend to occupy unusually large, irregular chambers — rooms that feel different from standard dungeon layouts.
Each boss guards a threshold. Defeating them may not drop treasure immediately, but the passage below opens.
Powerful monsters on deep floors often have specific weaknesses — study their resistances with the examine command (X).
The Gorgon's gaze is a magical attack. Items that resist magic or provide special vision may protect against it.
The Minotaur is a charging attacker — weapons with reach may let you strike before it reaches you.
The mightiest of Greek warriors, near-invulnerable, is hidden among the secret characters — golden armor and crimson plume mark an exceptional melee build.
A Spartan warrior who held a narrow pass against overwhelming odds is a hidden character — bronze helm, scarlet cape, and iron constitution define his legend.
A great conqueror in golden crown and royal purple cape, who never lost a battle, is hidden among the classes — a formidable all-around warrior build.
The hero who once slew the Minotaur walks as a hidden character — his helmet and noble lineage mark him as a balanced warrior who feels at home in labyrinths.
A winged divine messenger in silver-blue, gifted with unmatched speed and cunning, is a hidden rogue character — lighter on his feet than any other.
Ancient theology speaks of a second death — one the oldest texts say even Death cannot escape. Revelation may be the beginning of wisdom for those patient enough to observe, gather, and understand.
A riddle-asking guardian has been encountered around level 35. It offers a test before combat — those who pass gain an advantage; those who fail suffer for it.
A fire-breathing giant son of a forge god roams the mid-depths. Cold attacks cut through its resistance; its fire breath is devastating at close range.
The invulnerable lion of ancient myth appears in the dungeon. Blades and arrows are useless — it resists both entirely. Only crushing blows can harm it.
A Slavic iron-toothed witch heals as she fights and bites through armor. Iron and fire are her weaknesses — carry both if you enter her level.
A great serpent juvenile has been reported near the 55th level. Its venom is the most potent in the dungeon. Lightning and fire can wound it; nothing else reaches.
A chaos witch around level 40 drains magical power from those she touches. Stock up on MP before her floor. Holy and fire damage are effective.
An Egyptian jackal-servant drains life with shadow attacks. It dwells in darkness and is immune to drain itself. Holy attacks and lightning are your tools.
The knights of old sought the Grail in dungeons far below. The worthy must know the ways of faith.
Somewhere below the 30th level, an Oracle offers visions for tribute. Gold opens prophetic eyes.
A sealed tribunal near the 30th depth judges the wise. History holds the answers.
Jason brought back the Fleece by taming beasts, not slaying them. Animal knowledge is the key.
A well exists where wisdom can be purchased, but it demands an eye as payment.
Tiresias was blind yet saw more than any sighted prophet. Answer correctly while you cannot see.
Penelope wove and unwove her tapestry to delay her suitors. Constant armor work rewards patience.
The Valkyrie chose who died in battle. Ranged kills from a distance honor her precision.
Merlin learned his craft by casting unknown spells. Zap wands before you know their name.
Scheherazade spun tales from books unread. Read scrolls before identifying them.
Paracelsus, father of toxicology, held that the dose makes the poison. Let disease drain five points from your stats and learn what immunity truly costs.
Siegfried bathed in dragon blood to make himself invulnerable. Eat the cooked flesh of five distinct species that afflict with status effects.
Job endured every affliction without losing faith. Trigger five entirely different trap types hidden in locked containers.
Asclepius learned the arts of healing from serpents themselves. Harvest from fifteen different species capable of poisoning.
The Fisher King's wound could only be healed by the right question asked at the right time. Pray six times when barely any health remains.
Dionysus saw visions in every cup. Drink ten potions while your mind is already distorted by hallucination.
Apollo's arrows never missed. Hit the maximum possible chain length in combat ten times across separate battles.
Athena's owl observed all things that lived. Encounter fifty distinct monster species to earn the goddess's notice.
Loki wore a curse as easily as a disguise. Bear five different cursed items for at least ten turns each.
Thor named his hammer and never changed it. Fight thirty battles with the same weapon to forge a lasting bond with it.
Beowulf tore Grendel's arm off with bare hands. Win ten battles without any weapon equipped.
The Norns wove the thread of fate from memory and prophecy. Use Recall Lore twenty times.
Jormungandr encircled the world, always returning to itself. Equip and unequip the same weapon twenty times.
Shiva dances the dance of illusion that destroys and recreates the world. Spend one hundred turns in a hallucinating state.
Enkidu was born of the wilderness and knew every creature. Harvest successfully from twenty distinct monster species.
Circe transformed her guests through cuisine of impossible variety. Cook meals that cover five distinct effect categories.
Gawain's code demanded he fight even when bleeding out. Win six battles while below forty percent of your maximum health.
Kali's dance is patient and eternal — she slays one kind of creature without ever tiring. Slay one hundred of the same monster kind.
The Green Knight picked up his own severed head and walked away. Survive five separate hits each dealing thirty percent or more of your maximum health.
Narcissus gazed at his own reflection until it consumed him. Open and study your inventory thirty times.
Persephone mastered the bounty of two worlds. Cook five quality-5 meals, each from a distinct ingredient source.
5: The Philosopher's Stone lies at dungeon depth 100. Retrieve it and ascend to claim your victory.
A prayer at a sacred altar amplifies divine communion — Wisdom increases earned through prayer are permanent.
Abaddon, the final guardian, resists slash damage. Pierce weapons, blunt weapons, and powerful magic are your best options.
Some say the Minotaur's strength is tied to the labyrinth itself — cursed items found near its lair may be worth examining carefully.
Medusa's petrifying gaze is magical in nature. A ring of magic resistance or a wand of dispel can reduce its potency.
Fenrir's thick fur deflects most conventional attacks. Enchanted weapons with high chain multipliers hit hardest.
The deepest dungeon levels spawn void rations — consuming one before a boss fight sharpens combat reflexes dramatically.
A character who masters both Wisdom and Intelligence can cast powerful spells while enjoying generous quiz timers.
The three prayer boons available from the divine represent permanent Wisdom increases — a philosopher's greatest long-term investment.
Status effects on monsters can be exploited — a paralyzed boss is far easier to defeat, even briefly.
The man who catalogued the natural world and tutored a great conqueror is a hidden character — high INT and WIS, scholarly beard, and a blue robe mark his philosophical build.
A bald, bearded philosopher who famously claimed to know nothing is among the hidden characters — his extraordinary Wisdom provides the most generous quiz timers of any build.
A sage who wrote of caves, shadows, and ideal forms walks as a hidden philosopher character — high intellect, purple robe, and a beard that speaks of long contemplation.
A dark-robed philosopher who declared that suffering builds strength walks as a hidden character — his build is austere but psychologically formidable.
A legendary archmage in a star-studded dark robe carries a staff and begins already knowing powerful spells — the most magically gifted of all hidden characters.
A ranger of exceptional perception walks as a hidden character — forest green cloak, keen eyes, and a bow instead of a blade mark this sharp-sighted adventurer.
An ancient king who stood his ground at a narrow pass would be proud of any warrior who masters the dungeon depths — his secret build favors strength, endurance, and an unyielding spirit.
The Green Knight cannot be stopped by conventional means — he regenerates constantly. Fire or powerful magic are the only ways to stop his healing. Focus damage and push through.
The great whirlpool beast pulls you toward it every turn. Lightning deals heavy damage to it. Do not stand still — keep moving or its maw will grind you to nothing.
A severed arm of the demon king Ravana attacks three times each round. Fire and holy damage are its weaknesses. It can paralyze — carry a wand of dispel or a potion of freedom.
The Wendigo drains stamina at terrifying speed — you will begin to starve mid-fight. Fire is its only true weakness. Eat before engaging and carry food.
The Wild Hunt Captain leads spectral riders. Iron weapons penetrate his defenses; holy energy disrupts his phantom nature. He is fast — do not let him surround you.
Anansi never fights fairly. He confuses and poisons, teleporting his prey into disorientation. Fire burns his webs. Lightning disrupts his trickery. Do not trust your footing.
A fragment of Níðhöggr carries acid that ignores most armor. Holy damage and lightning hurt it. Its poison is near-permanent without magical remedy — bring a wand of cure.
Perseus held his shield as a mirror to defeat what direct sight would destroy. Block an enemy's status attack and reflect it back — do this five times.
Theseus mapped every inch of the labyrinth before he struck. Fully explore five entire dungeon floors, leaving no passage unseen.
Hermes moved between worlds so swiftly the gods lost track of him. Use a scroll of teleportation eight times.
The Sibyl of Cumae spoke ten thousand prophecies. Answer five hundred questions correctly before reaching the twentieth depth.
Ahasverus walked forever and never rested. Fifteen thousand steps in the dungeon are said to reveal all hidden paths.
Ariadne gave her thread so the hero could escape swiftly. Leave ten dungeon floors within thirty turns of arriving on each.
Morgan le Fay cast her most powerful spells from the edge of ruin. Cast a spell six times when below twenty percent of your maximum health.
Cu Chulainn — Cuchulainn — the Irish hero whose battle-fury was triggered by fear. Win five battles while under the feared condition.
Sisyphus' boulder rests on a steep slope near the 80th depth. The Fates respect those who bear it willingly.
The Fisher King waits to be healed. A herb found near him and the right questions of faith will restore him — and reward you.
Near the 60th depth, an ancient king waits. The herb of healing grows on his floor.
Odin hung on the World Tree for nine days to gain wisdom. A similar vigil in the dungeon is said to grant sight of all things.
The Mithridates Protocol: be poisoned, then eat what poisoned you, five times across five species.
Prometheus burned forever and yet lived. Survive ten separate bleeding wounds of five turns each.
Ragnarok's survivor descended to the deepest floor with barely a heartbeat remaining.
